title: Hematemesis - Upper GI Bleed
chief_complaint: Vomiting blood (coffee ground and fresh red) for 3 hours, black tarry stools. Known NSAID use for chronic back pain.

hidden_data.endoscopy: Duodenal ulcer with visible vessel, Forrest IIa

patient_responses.vomiting: I vomited dark blood first, then bright red
patient_responses.nsaid_use: I take ibuprofen every day for my back pain
patient_responses.stools: My stools have been black and sticky for 2 days

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! UGIB from duodenal ulcer. The elevated BUN was a key clue.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is an upper GI bleed. The BUN:Cr ratio and melena point to UGIB.
feedback.attending_note: UGIB management: 1) Resuscitate with IV fluids, 2) IV PPI 80mg bolus then 8mg/hr, 3) Endoscopy within 24h, 4) Stop NSAIDs. Good case, Doctor.
